title: The Design of Everyday Things
author: Don Norman
notes: Mental models, affordances, and the gulfs of execution / evaluation.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
notes: Science-focused sci-fi. Ryland Grace is a great narrator.

title: The Making of the Atomic Bomb
author: Richard Rhodes
notes: Long, dense, excellent. Chapter on Bohr is the best I've read on him.

title: Domain-Driven Design
author: Eric Evans
notes: Aggregates and bounded contexts still age well. Chapters 1-5 do most of the work.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
notes: System 1 vs 2 is the core; the second half on happiness is weaker.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andy Hunt, Dave Thomas
notes: Still the best single book to put in a junior dev's hands.

title: Chip War
author: Chris Miller
notes: Everyone I trust has recommended this.

title: Seeing Like a State
author: James C. Scott
notes: Legibility, high-modernist schemes, and why top-down simplifications fail.

title: A Fire Upon the Deep
author: Vernor Vinge
notes: Couldn't stay engaged with the Zones premise. Might retry.

title: Surely You're Joking, Mr. Feynman!
author: Richard Feynman
notes: The curiosity is infectious. Best chapter: the safe-cracking at Los Alamos.
